title: Reis book
wikitext:
[]: Rei's book             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Apr 20, 2008 11:52 am --

[]:             Quote Post by majlund » Sun Apr 20, 2008 1:01 pm Here goes... -#1---------- Count Ludwig von Anhalt spoke at an assembly of a language society: so to the honour and beauty of our old mother tongue, that, like we wish to read or write, from our own and not from borrowed reserves we take. It has for many years been a habit, and by many still is, to beleive one can't properly read or write unless all kinds of Latin, French or Italian words are also mixed in. ------------- -#2---------- with thousands of sides / laying claim to/ or otherwise due to luck become manly and known / equal to knightly lords / and noble persons of ancient families / - ; though often / their ancestors simple workers / pullers and load carriers : their fathers mule drivers: their brothers (lawyers) and (executioners): their sisters whores : their mothers procurers and even witches: and in sum / their entire kin from everywhere / stained and soiled / despite all the coating and luxury in its splendour; yes these / these "nobles" / are often so black / as if they were born and raised i Guinea. -------------- The usual disclaimers for translations apply I've tried to capture the spirit of the text - maybe at the cost of readability.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Apr 20, 2008 2:14 pm So the text is completely unrelated to the pictures? ={o

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Apr 20, 2008 3:17 pm Big thanks to Zuggy and majlund for this text extraction and translation work. Intriguing stuff, though methinks we're looking at another History of Gainix incident.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Apr 20, 2008 3:36 pm --

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Sun Apr 20, 2008 5:19 pm It's ust words on a page...just words. I suppose the Genetics could play a part..... but it's mostly just words. It can't be a blank page, so they fill it with half-nonsense from a book laying around. Star Trek does the same thing with the Okuda-grams..... I think there's a Porsche 911 on the Enterprise D, according to the plan in Engineeringg I'll spare the random rant on the Lisbon Chain that I'd originally written here for some weird reason.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Apr 20, 2008 5:26 pm FWIW: here are the thread (just did a search for "Rei book") http://www.evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=782 I'll address the forum topics in another thread.

[]:             Quote Post by RandomStuff » Sun Apr 20, 2008 9:01 pm Any idea why so much of the stuff in eva in German?

[]:             Quote Post by majlund » Mon Apr 21, 2008 7:52 am Zuggy wrote: Is #2 supposed to be from a poem do you think? I was wondering if the /'s were supposed to mark where each new line began.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Mon Apr 21, 2008 9:33 am --

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Mon Apr 21, 2008 4:17 pm Maybe Rei was trying to learn german so she could get closer to Asuka in her own weird way......??????

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Mon Apr 21, 2008 4:20 pm Using WWII IJN vessel names could, I agree, indicate that Mr. Anno was using German, as Germany and Japan were pretty close during those times (maybe using names of ships sunk is trying to make an implication, or am I thinking too much)?

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Mon Apr 21, 2008 4:30 pm If there is in fact a connection between Eva and the appearance of 400 year-old German text, it may be through the 400 year-old German Athanasius Kircher (1602-1680). The "Systema Sephiroticum" diagram used in the opening credits was from his studies of the Kabbalah. Considering the fact the Chairman of the Instrumentality Committee has a German name, that GEHIRN, NERV, and SEELE are all German words, and that two of NERV's branches are in Germany (Misato is said to have joined GEHIRN through its Hamburg branch, not one in Japan), it seems reasonable to assume a strong German connection in Evangelion , even beyond everyone's favorite Aryan*, Asuka. --C. *Half-Aryan, actually, just like in Tezuka's Adolf .

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Apr 21, 2008 5:11 pm Zuggy wrote: I thought I had put the title as "Rei's boob" for a moment there.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Apr 21, 2008 5:30 pm Zuggy wrote: Japan sided with Germany during WWII, correct?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Apr 21, 2008 5:38 pm At the risk of repeating myself -- for further reading look up the Siebold Society, founded by Philipp Franz von Siebold (1796-1866) and chaired at one time by Dr. Constantin von Brandenstein-Zeppelin (a plausible candidate to be Asuka's maternal grandfather).

[]:             Quote Post by universalperson » Mon Apr 21, 2008 8:13 pm BobBQ wrote: Zuggy wrote: Japan sided with Germany during WWII, correct?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Apr 21, 2008 8:22 pm Don't worry. It's not even a millionth of what he knows about guns.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Tue Apr 22, 2008 12:38 pm Firearms is still interesting though.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Wed Apr 23, 2008 2:24 am Bobo's talk about guns is an acquired taste. You get used to it, and then you get interested...
